purpose: Principal-grade KG. Each case: war story (setup/conflict/decision/result/lesson), layered citation (public/NDA/private), industry benchmark intel gained, patent linkages.

citation_layers.public: Citable on any platform call, LinkedIn, written DD. No NDA risk. Default for screening.
citation_layers.nda_with_buyer: Citable in DD under executed buyer NDA. Specific numbers, named scopes. Not for unverified platforms.
citation_layers.private_retainer: Inside retainer engagements with signed scope only. Mechanism-level detail, internal names, undisclosed decisions.

cases.title: Project Director, $550M CapEx program, FEL 1 to FEL 3

scale.note: Aggregate program scope; single-year disclosed CapEx is fraction

war_story.setup: Owner-side mandate to take a $550M non-ferrous program from FEL 1 through FEL 3 to FEED-ready, stage-gated through internal IC.
war_story.conflict: FEL 2 estimate vs EPC market pricing diverged at FEL 3 entry. Stage-gate at risk.
war_story.decision: Restructured contracting strategy and tightened scope-freeze discipline before authorising detailed engineering.
war_story.result: FEL 3 closure into IC submission within window.
war_story.lesson: FEL 2 to 3 transition is where 60% of CapEx overruns are seeded. Stage-gate discipline matters more than estimate accuracy.

industry_intel_gained: Russian/CIS EPC market pricing dynamics 2023-2025, delta to international benchmark
FEL 3 readiness criteria (process design freeze, P&ID issued, long-lead PO authorisations)
How IC committees in Russian industrial groups read FEL 3 packages

citation.public: Project Director on a $550M CapEx program at UMMC, FEL 1 through FEL 3.
citation.nda_with_buyer: Specific contracting strategy, scope-freeze methodology, stage-gate criteria.
citation.private_retainer: Internal IC dynamics, named stakeholders, gate decisions.

cases.title: FEL Director, gold-from-tailings greenfield, ~50 bn RUB

scale.note: Greenfield gold recovery from historical tailings

war_story.setup: Historical tailings with residual gold above marginal cut-off. Greenfield retreatment plant, FEL 1.
war_story.conflict: Cyanide leach kinetics on weathered tailings diverged from fresh-ore lab data. Recovery curve flatter.
war_story.decision: Reordered FEL 2 testwork to prioritise grind-vs-recovery over throughput-vs-CapEx.
war_story.result: FEL 3 entered with corrected metallurgical balance.
war_story.lesson: On tailings retreatment, weathering chemistry breaks ore-body analogies. Insist on aged-sample testwork before FEL 2 closure.

industry_intel_gained: Aged tailings cyanide leach kinetics: 35-55% recovery vs 75-90% on fresh ore at same grind
Detox circuit sizing for tailings retreatment, SO2/air vs hydrogen peroxide
Permitting nuances for tailings retreatment in RF (re-classification as ore source)

citation.public: Directed FEL 1-3 on a gold-from-tailings greenfield, ~50 bn RUB, at UMMC.
citation.private_retainer: Named consultants, equipment vendors, FID economics.

cases.title: Casting line modernisation, 40 ktpa, CapEx 18% below benchmark

war_story.setup: 40 ktpa casting expansion at operating smelter — VDC billet, degassing, CFF filtration. FEL 2 closed.
war_story.conflict: EPC bid 22% above FEL 2 estimate. IC window 4 weeks.
war_story.decision: Broke EPC into 6 discipline-specific tenders (civil/structural/mechanical/electrical/automation/refractory). Integration engineering in-house.
war_story.result: CapEx closed 18% below benchmark. Became contracting model for two subsequent projects.
war_story.lesson: Single-EPC pricing carries 15-25% interface premium on brownfield expansions. Owner team with metallurgical depth can disintermediate. Without bench — single EPC is correct.

industry_intel_gained: Casthouse CapEx benchmark VDC billet: $1100-1500/t annual capacity (2018-2020 USD)
Discipline tendering vs single EPC: break-even in internal engineering hours
CFF filter sizing and replacement economics, AlSi vs AlMg
TiBor grain refiner dosing vs final billet quality

citation.public: On a 40 ktpa casting expansion at RUSAL I led a contracting restructure that closed CapEx 18% below industry benchmark.
citation.nda_with_buyer: Tender decomposition, integration engineering scope, in-source/out-source split.
citation.private_retainer: Vendor names, unit pricing, IC deck.

cases.title: CapEx portfolio, 12 projects, $350M+ aggregate

war_story.setup: 12 active projects: smelting (anode plant, potroom upgrades, busbar modernisation), casthouse (multiple lines), downstream (rolling, foil).
war_story.conflict: Portfolio-level resource conflicts. Common process engineering pool. Sequential IC submissions creating queue.
war_story.decision: Tier-based FEL pace: Tier-1 strategic = full FEL 1-3; Tier-2 sustaining = accelerated FEL 2 to FID; Tier-3 replacement = engineering-led scope, bypassed formal FEL.
war_story.result: IC clearances per year doubled without expanding engineering headcount.
war_story.lesson: Not every CapEx needs full FEL. Replacement/sustaining with low scope variability is over-served by full stage-gating. Strategic/growth is under-served by anything less.

industry_intel_gained: Tier-based FEL methodology — project tier vs stage-gate intensity
Aluminium downstream sustaining CapEx norms (rolling rolls, foil annealers, packaging)
Anode plant CapEx benchmarks (green vs baked anode)
Internal IC dynamics in vertically integrated aluminium groups

citation.public: Led a CapEx portfolio of 12 projects, $350M+ aggregate, across the aluminium value chain at RUSAL.
citation.nda_with_buyer: Tier-based FEL methodology, per-tier scope decisions.
citation.private_retainer: Project list, stage outcomes, IC voting.

cases.title: Operational turnaround, 305 staff, zero LTI through campaign

war_story.setup: Operational turnaround mandate, downstream refining, multi-shift, 305 direct reports + contractor labour.
war_story.conflict: HSE incident frequency trending up. Production targets requiring overtime. Stress cycle.
war_story.decision: Inverted priority: HSE first, throughput second, cost third. Used 90-day window to retrain supervision on critical-control verification before pushing throughput.
war_story.result: Zero LTI through campaign. Throughput targets met by month 5.
war_story.lesson: Turnarounds done HSE-throughput-cost succeed. Reverse order eventually pays the safety bill back with interest.

industry_intel_gained: Norilsk Nickel refining flowsheet (matte → chloride/carbonyl → cathode Ni, Cu, Co, PGM)
Critical-control framework for non-ferrous refining (energy isolation, molten metal, process gas)
PGM stream commercial dynamics, anode slimes economics
Russian Arctic operational constraints (logistics windows, manpower retention)

citation.public: Led an operational turnaround at Norilsk Nickel, 305 staff under direct management, zero LTI through campaign.
citation.nda_with_buyer: HSE intervention methodology, throughput recovery sequence.
citation.private_retainer: Site identification, stakeholder names, KPI baselines.

cases.title: CapEx portfolio transformation, internal lead, McKinsey workstream

scale.note: Group-level CapEx portfolio, multi-asset Kazakhstan operations

war_story.setup: Group CapEx transformation with McKinsey. Internal team paired with consultant pod. Multi-asset: ferrochrome (Aktobe/Aksu), alumina (Pavlodar), iron ore, coal.
war_story.conflict: Consultant playbook assumed standard FEL. Group reality: legacy projects mid-execution, mixed Soviet-era and Western methodology, speed over rigour.
war_story.decision: Built hybrid methodology: clean structure for new work, separate legacy track for in-flight projects.
war_story.result: Transformation closed. Methodology adopted on subsequent CapEx waves.
war_story.lesson: Greenfield consultant methodology cannot be force-fitted onto brownfield portfolios. Hybrid track design is the deliverable.

industry_intel_gained: Kazakhstan non-ferrous regulatory environment, subsoil law, contract-mining
Ferrochrome CapEx benchmarks (Aktobe vs international peers)
Alumina refinery process and CapEx norms (Bayer digestion intensity)
How McKinsey/Bain/BCG run mining CapEx engagements — internal team interface

citation.public: Internal lead on a CapEx portfolio transformation at ERG, conducted with McKinsey.

cases.title: Independent DD, 250 ktpa aluminium asset Kazakhstan, deal killed

war_story.setup: PE fund considering 250 ktpa aluminium smelter in Kazakhstan. Stated expansion CapEx, asserted energy cost stability. Two-week DD window.
war_story.conflict: Public-source review of regional energy tariff trajectory and asset power contract structure didn't match management representation.
war_story.decision: Wrote up energy-cost exposure as primary deal risk. Listed three killer questions for management session.
war_story.result: Client walked from deal after management could not answer killer questions.
war_story.lesson: On commodities assets the metallurgy is rarely the deal-killer. Power contract structure is. Read the power agreement before the metallurgy.

industry_intel_gained: Kazakhstan power tariff escalation mechanism for industrial consumers
Aluminium smelter cost-curve sensitivity to power tariff delta
PE buyer-side decision dynamics on DD red flags

citation.public: Conducted independent DD on a 250 ktpa aluminium asset in Kazakhstan; client walked from deal after DD-identified risks were unanswered at management session.
citation.nda_with_buyer: DD methodology, killer questions, full risk register.
citation.private_retainer: Client identity, asset identity, deal economics.

cases.title: Expert call, aluminium smelter turnaround risks, GLG

war_story.setup: GLG dispatched call from international IB modelling aluminium asset for IPO advisory.
war_story.conflict: Their valuation model assumed turnaround duration and CapEx not matching operational reality.
war_story.decision: Used three reference turnaround windows to triangulate realistic durations and provisioning.
war_story.result: Client adjusted model. Confirmed via platform follow-up.
war_story.lesson: Bank analysts modelling commodities universally under-provision for turnarounds. Structurally consistent. Pricing-relevant for DD.

industry_intel_gained: Aluminium potline turnaround duration norms: planned 22-30 days, unplanned recovery 60-90 days
Potline relining CapEx benchmark per pot type and current density
How IB analysts model turnaround CapEx and why they get it wrong

citation.public: Expert call via GLG on aluminium smelter turnaround dynamics, supported a major IB's IPO advisory modelling.

cases.title: Vendor DD and negotiation, Chinese anode furnace, 12% price reduction

war_story.setup: Client engaged Chinese anode furnace OEM for downstream installation. Initial bid above budget. Client lacked in-house benchmark.
war_story.conflict: Standard OEM one-price negotiation. Client risked accepting or losing supplier.
war_story.decision: Built benchmark from three peer references + one internal reference. Decomposed total price into core, auxiliaries, services, spares. Identified two soft components where Chinese vendors over-price for Western buyers.
war_story.result: 12% price reduction, extended warranty, free commissioning.
war_story.lesson: Chinese OEM pricing for non-Chinese buyers carries 10-20% 'unfamiliar buyer' premium that disappears with credible benchmark.

industry_intel_gained: Anode furnace pricing structure (core/auxiliaries/services/spares splits)
Chinese OEM negotiation dynamics — what they will and will not move on
Warranty norms 12mo vs 24mo and what 'extended' actually unlocks

citation.public: Advised aluminium producer on a Chinese anode furnace supplier negotiation, 12% price reduction and extended warranty.
citation.nda_with_buyer: Benchmark methodology, decomposition framework, negotiation script.

anti_cases.bridge_template_en: My direct experience is in [X], not [Y], but the [Z] dynamics across non-ferrous projects are structurally similar. I can comment on [bridge topic]. For [Y]-specific data I'd recommend a colleague.

nickel_refining.anode_slime_pgm_grade: varies; Norilsk feed PGM-rich vs global

factual_guardrails_critical: UMMC gold-from-tailings is GOLD, not copper. Distinct from Udokan (USM).
Udokan = USM entity. Anton applied there but no engagement.
RUSAL portfolio = aluminium only. UMMC ≠ aluminium for Anton's role.
$550M UMMC = TOTAL PROGRAM SCOPE multi-phase. Single-year disclosed CapEx is a fraction. Frame as 'program scope'.
7 patents = all RUSAL employer, aluminium domain.
0 LTI at Norilsk Nickel = within Anton's 305-staff campaign, not group-wide.
Knowledge Ridge = one paid call + one pro-bono article only. Not active revenue.
Anton has NEVER worked at: Rio Tinto, BHP, Glencore, Vale, EGA, Alcoa, Hydro, RUSAL Guinea subsidiary. Don't claim adjacent.
